compiled_state.title: Implement hook_record_skill_attribution + create post-skill.sh + register hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Three sequential sub-steps (each independently deployable): (1) Add hook_record_skill_attribution(input_json) to plugins/dso/hooks/lib/post-functions.sh — same config guard pattern as T1b; appends {"type":"skill","skill_name":"<value>"} to JSONL; (2) Create plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-skill.sh following exact pattern of post-agent.sh (trap exit 0, resolves CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, sources dispatcher.sh + post-functions.sh, calls _run_post_fn hook_record_skill_attribution "$INPUT"); chmod +x; (3) Register post-skill.sh in .claude/settings.json PostToolUse list for Skill tool. AC: (a) function appends skill entry when attribution.enabled=true; (b) test -x post-skill.sh; (c) end-to-end test: execute post-skill.sh with mock INPUT env var and assert JSONL entry written (behavioral test, not content-grep); (d) all T2a tests pass; (e) validate.sh --ci passes. Note: test -x is a deployment property check (executable bit), not a prohibited existence-only check.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: Hook registration belongs in plugin.json, not .claude/settings.json.

The .claude/settings.json file only contains enabledPlugins — it is not where individual hook handlers are registered. The actual hook registration for this plugin lives in plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json under the 'hooks.PostToolUse' array.

Amended step 3: 'Register post-skill.sh in plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json under hooks.PostToolUse as a new matcher entry for the Skill tool, following the existing pattern for the Agent tool matcher (look for the existing PostToolUse handler for Agent tool in plugin.json and add an equivalent entry for Skill).'

If the hook is added to .claude/settings.json instead, the Skill tool PostToolUse hook will never fire — the plugin hook system reads from plugin.json.
comments.author: Test
